rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc. And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFFs CECIL STEPHENS and DAVID STEPHENS' Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc. will be heard in Department 503 at 1:30pm, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Plaintiff¿s motion is denied. Plaintiff concedes that its motion to compel served in Cecil Stephens¿ underlying personal injury action was untimely, thus plaintiff is barred from seeking identical relief in this wrongful death action. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 1:30pm. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Bell Industries, Inc.S Motion To Compel Further Responses T Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 And Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JUNE 8, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Bell Industries, Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory no. 17.1 and requests for production, and request for sanctions is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Friedman¿S Home Improvement And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFFs CECIL STEPHENS and DAVID STEPHENS MOTION Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Friedman's Home Improvement will be heard in Department 503 at 1:30pm, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Plaintiff¿s motion is granted. Defendant waived its objections to the deposition notice by filing them late. C.C.P. Section 2025.410(a). Defendant did not substantively oppose plaintiff¿s motion. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions of $1000 is granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 1:30pm. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL STEPHENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'S Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Requests For Admission; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 8, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:15 a.m. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s further responses to special interrogatories, requests for production of documents and requests for admission will be heard in Department 503 at 1:30pm, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Plaintiff¿s motion is denied. Defendant previously responded to duplicative discovery in Cecil Stephens¿ underlying personal injury action. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 1:30pm. = (503/TLJ)
